summary: No-ship: the committed RED record matches red.sha and both tests fail for the intended PASS ABORTED symptom, but Round 21’s blocking logging witness is not wired into executable acceptance. Simpler alternative: none materially safer for the production fix. Open question: none.

findings.title: Blocking W-LOG witnesses can remain undiscovered
findings.body: The exhaustive test-change scope omits `src/store/pins/tests/log_witness.rs` and its required `mod log_witness;` registration, although W-LOG-D is declared blocking elsewhere. Rust does not auto-discover files under `src/store/pins/tests`; therefore every broad Cargo command can report 0 failed without compiling W-LOG-A/B/C/D, leaving the documented logging mutants alive.
findings.recommendation: Add `log_witness.rs` and `mod log_witness;` explicitly to B-1 and Scope. Make acceptance verify that every W-LOG test ID is discovered before running the suite, so zero discovered tests cannot pass.

next_steps: Wire W-LOG-A/B/C/D into the Rust module tree and B-1 scope.
Add a discovery assertion for the W-LOG test IDs, then repeat the plan review without executing GREEN tests.
